title: Delta Air Lines Debuts Sustainable Aviation Fuel at Detroit Metro Airport
content: For the first time in Michigan aviation history, sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) has arrived at Detroit Metropolitan Airport for use on Delta Air Lines flights.SAF is a drop-in-solution that can be used in todayâ€™s aircraft without any modifications, and it can reduce lifecycle carbon emissions by up to 80 percent compared to traditional petroleum-powered jet fuel, with the potential to vastly reduce the aviation industryâ€™s carbon footprint, according to Delta.The SAF shipment of more than 7,000 gallons was delivered via existing pipeline directly to DTW this week with the collaboration of multiple partners. The SAF batch was initially transported by Shell from Montana Renewables (MRL), the largest producer of SAF in North America, to a Buckeye Partners facility in Dearborn.
author: Analysts Account
